_meta.description: Five test queries for the RAG retrieval comparison cell in 03_rag_pipeline.ipynb. Each query is annotated with the paper and section that contains the ground-truth answer. Metric: hit@k = 1 if any of the top-k retrieved chunks comes from the ground-truth source section, else 0.

questions.question: What are the three steps in a single iteration of the ReAct loop?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Each iteration produces a Thought (reasoning trace), an Action (tool call), and an Observation (tool result fed back into context). They interleave for several rounds until the model emits a final answer.

questions.question: Why is scaled dot-product attention preferred over additive attention for transformer architectures?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Dot-product attention is faster and more space-efficient in practice because it can be implemented as highly optimised matrix multiplication, even though additive attention has slightly better theoretical performance for large dimensions without scaling.

questions.question: According to the Chinchilla paper, what is the optimal ratio of training tokens to model parameters?
questions.expected_answer_summary: For compute-optimal training, model size and training tokens should scale roughly equally — about 20 tokens per parameter, far more data than prior scaling laws suggested.

questions.question: What is the role of the AI feedback signal in Constitutional AI training?
questions.expected_answer_summary: The AI generates self-critiques and revisions against a written constitution; these self-revisions train a preference model that then provides reward signal during RL fine-tuning, replacing human preference labels.

questions.question: What are the two main components of the retrieval-augmented generation architecture?
questions.expected_answer_summary: A non-parametric retriever (DPR-based dense passage retrieval over a Wikipedia index) and a parametric generator (a seq2seq model like BART) that conditions on both the query and the retrieved passages.
